Brokers Digest: Local Equities - Banking sector, Yinson Holdings Bhd, MN Holdings Bhd, Sapura Energy Bhd
Banking Sector OVERWEIGHT
data.description:
This article first appeared in Capital, The Edge Malaysia Weekly on April 7, 2025 - April 13, 2025

Banking Sector

OVERWEIGHT

RHB RESEARCH (APRIL 2): Bank Negara Malaysia’s February 2025 statistics pointed to continued strength in the banking system. Despite a slight month-on-month (m-o-m) moderation in loan growth, asset quality continued to improve while regulatory liquidity indicators stayed healthy. We remain upbeat on the sector and prefer banking stocks that are trading at reasonable valuations, with solid earnings and/or dividend growth prospects.

System loans expanded 5.2% y-o-y (flat m-o-m) in February, with loans to the household sector leading the charge (+6% y-o-y, +1% m-o-m). This mainly stemmed from a strong rise in loans for residential mortgages (+7% y-o-y, flat m-o-m) as well as hire purchase loans (+8% y-o-y, flat m-o-m).

Non-household loan growth was a more modest 4% y-o-y (flat m-o-m), with the finance (+18% y-o-y, -1% m-o-m) and real estate (+4% y-o-y, flat m-o-m) segments as key drivers.

We maintain our system loan growth forecast of about 5% y-o-y for 2025, premised on resilient growth in the domestic economy and the gradual execution of various national development master plans.

Demand for credit still looks robust as system loan applications are up 7% y-o-y YTD, mainly thanks to the non-household segment. Meanwhile, YTD system loan approvals rose by a smaller 1% y-o-y, while disbursements are down 3% y-o-y. We think this is indicative of a step-up in disbursements to come, which is supportive of loan growth down the road.

System deposits gained 3% y-o-y (m-o-m: +1%) in February. Current accounts and savings accounts (CASA) continued to be the preferred deposit channel as these increased by a stronger 4% y-o-y (m-o-m: +1%) — the CASA ratio rose slightly to 31.4% (+0.2ppts y-o-y and m-o-m) as a result.

Despite a tighter loan-to-deposit-ratio of 87% (+1ppt y-o-y), Bank Negara does not believe there is a liquidity crunch in the system, as the liquidity coverage ratio remains at healthy levels (February 2025: 154%, January 2025: 158%, February 2024: 154%).

We have “buy” calls for our top picks AMMB Holdings Bhd (KL:AMBANK), CIMB Group Holdings Bhd (KL:CIMB) and Hong Leong Bank Bhd (KL:HLBANK), with target prices (TPs) of RM6.70, RM9.25 and RM26.60 respectively.

Yinson Holdings Bhd

Target price: RM3.15 OUTPERFORM

KENANGA RESEARCH(APRIL 2): Yinson Holdings Bhd’s (KL:YINSON) financial year 2025 (FY2025) results came in slightly below expectations due to higher-than-expected interest costs. Core earnings improved as new floating production, storage and offloading (FPSO) projects (FPSO Maria Quiteria and FPSO Atlanta) achieved first oil in 4QFY2025. While the engineering, procurement, construction, installation and commissioning losses are non-core, we believe the cost overruns incurred during the FPSO conversion stage suggest weaker project economics during the leasing phase, as the overall capital expenditure (capex) base has exceeded initial expectations.

Hence, we reduce our TP by 19% to RM3.15 (from RM3.87) after cutting FY26 and FY27 forecast earnings to account for lower margins for the new FPSO projects and remain “outperform” on the stock.

We cut FY26 and FY27F earnings by 18% and 17% respectively to account for lower Ebitda margins from FPSO Maria Quiteria and FPSO Atlanta in the light of higher operating costs expected for the projects, to tone down our profit expectations for both projects due to cost inflation as well as higher interest cost assumptions.

Correspondingly, we reduce our SOP-derived TP by 19% to RM3.15 from RM3.87 as a result of lower margins assumed for FPSO Atlanta and FPSO Maria Quiteria, with adjustment made for all FPSO DCF as we roll forward the valuation by a year. 

MN Holdings Bhd

Target price: RM1.60 BUY

PHILLIP CAPITAL (APRIL 2): MN Holdings Bhd (KL:MNHLDG) has secured a RM138 million contract from Tenaga Nasional Bhd (KL:TENAGA) to establish a new 275kV transmission main intake at the Kenyir Switching Station, featuring a double-busbar configuration, located at the Kenyir Hydro Power Station in Terengganu. The contract duration is for 24 months, with works expected to commence in March 2025 and be completed by March 2027.

This is the largest contract secured from Tenaga, a testament to its expertise in high-voltage power infrastructure projects and its positioning to benefit from Tenaga’s rising capex under Regulatory Period 4 (RP4). Including this win, year-to-date job wins of RM884 million have exceeded our previous full-year replenishment target of RM700 million.

MN’s outstanding order book stands at RM978 million, which implies a strong cover of 3.8 times FY24 revenue, providing strong earnings visibility over the next two years. Assuming a 7% profit after tax margin, we project that this contract will contribute about RM10 million in Patami over FY26 and FY27E.

Looking ahead, the tender pipeline remains strong at RM1.4 billion across various sectors, including Tenaga/Sarawak Energy Bhd (37%), data centre (37%), solar (10%), water and sewerage (3%) and others (12%).

Sapura Energy Bhd

Target price: 7 sen BUY

UOB KAY HIAN (MARCH 28): Sapura Energy Bhd’s (KL:SAPNRG) FY25 core loss missed expectations as its rigs were not spared the industry’s slowdown towards end-2024. This was a period of market correction for the global oil and gas industry, against the overheating rise in vessel charter rates and oil price uncertainties. This adversely impacted rigs Sapura T-9 (no contract) and Sapura Pelaut (work postponed to FY26).

Ebitda margins for rigs were among the worst in recent history at 35%. Still, near-term rig utilisation remains under stress. Sapura T-17’s contract expired last December, rendering it idle for a month at the very least in 4QFY25, with the possibility of its remaining jobless until July. Sapura Berani, idle since February, may commence work for ExxonMobil in May.

The group orderbook implies FY2026/FY2027 revenue recognitions of RM4 billion/RM1.5 billion respectively, and does not include the joint venture orderbook of RM5.5 billion (compared with RM5.8 billion qoq). Sapura Energy is actively bidding again, with a bidbook total of RM37 billion.

Our TP assumes the successful sale of SapuraOMV (RM2.3 billion valuation) with: (a) RM1.1 billion white knight support (whose identity will remain undisclosed until certain milestones are reached; and (b) improving free cash flow, largely due to the pipelay support vessel.
